title: input_code_editor(): caret is still off-set
rationale: Facts: Reporter lgaborini provides a reprex (input_code_editor() inside layout_columns(), R shiny 1.14.0, bslib 0.11.0.9000 dev build from GitHub commit 996f77e installed same day) plus a screenshot showing the visual caret drifting right of the true insertion point on a line with no trailing space. No comments yet. Classification hypothesis: duplicate/reoccurrence of a previously 'fixed' defect rather than a fresh bug. Duplicate search: found rstudio/bslib#1303 (closed 2026-04-29), same title pattern and identical reprex, fixed by PR #1304 which added `.prism-code-editor` to the font-family CSS selector so bslib's monospace font wins over a prism theme's own font-family declaration. Wrong-location check: bslib is correct — input_code_editor() and its CSS ship from this repo. Regression check: not labeled regression because I cannot cite a bisected commit that reintroduced the defect; instead this is evidence that PR #1304's fix is incomplete — the new report uses the default theme (no `theme=` argument), whereas #1303's root cause was specifically non-default prism themes overriding font-family, so either the default theme has its own unaddressed font-family gap or a different regression has occurred since #1304. Reproduction plan: run the reporter's reprex (input_code_editor with default theme, R/bslib dev build) and type a line of code; observe caret drifting ahead of the real cursor position, matching the linked screenshot. Impact: affects any input_code_editor() user regardless of custom theme, i.e. broader than the originally patched case, but purely cosmetic/visual (text content itself is correct). Priority: Medium — real, reproducible UX bug in a promoted component with a known root-cause area, but not data-loss or blocking. Recommended next action: maintainers should verify whether the default theme's CSS also needs a font-family override, or bisect since PR #1304 to see if another change reintroduced the drift; do not close as a pure duplicate without confirming the fix scope, since the previous fix targeted a narrower case (custom themes only).

title: bs_dependency_defer()  factory-built closures collide on the memoise cache
rationale: Facts: Reporter AleKoure provides a minimal reprex showing that bs_dependency_defer()'s memoise cache key is derived from formals(f)+body(f)+call args; factory-built closures (the pattern shown in the 'Dynamically themeable component' vignette) only differ in captured variables, so distinct components hash to the same key and every dependency after the first silently reuses the first one's compiled CSS output. COLLABORATOR cpsievert replied same-day: 'those suggestions seem pretty reasonable...feel free to submit a PR', confirming the defect and inviting a fix. Classification hypothesis: bug (cache-key collision); no classification label in labels.yaml fits a generic 'bug' so none is applied beyond priority. Duplicate check: searched bslib issues/PRs for 'bs_dependency_defer', 'memoise cache' — no other open or closed issue covers this. Wrong-location check: bslib is correct, bs_dependency_defer() is defined here. Regression check: not applicable — this is a longstanding design limitation of the memoise key, not a behavior change from a prior release. Reproduction plan: run the reporter's mk_dep() reprex creating two factory-built dependencies with different CSS bodies and confirm both report the first dependency's name/output. Impact: silently serves the wrong (first-registered) compiled dependency to every subsequent factory-built themeable component sharing the same generic wrapper signature — a correctness bug with no error surfaced, though it only affects the advanced 'dynamically themeable component' authoring pattern documented in the vignette; workaround (memoise=FALSE) exists and is already known to the reporter. Priority: Medium — confirmed real bug with maintainer buy-in and a viable workaround, matching the rubric's 'valid bug...moderate impact or a reasonable workaround' guidance. Recommended next action: accept the reporter's offered PR adding an optional cache_key argument to bs_dependency_defer(), and update the 'Dynamically themeable component' vignette to warn about the shared-cache collision for factory-built func.

title: feat: Add `render.download_button` and `render.download_link`
rationale: Facts: MEMBER schloerke (self-filed) proposes splitting Express's auto-rendered download UI into two explicit renderers, render.download_button and render.download_link, mirroring the existing render.text/render.code 1:1 renderer-to-output-component precedent (#997); includes a same-day follow-up comment showing the current clunky workaround (importing download_link from Core, wrapping @render.download in ui.hold()) versus the proposed simplified Express app. Classification hypothesis: feature/API ergonomics enhancement, self-scoped by a maintainer. Duplicate check: searched for 'download_link'/'download_button' issues — only a closed, unrelated doc-link issue (#555); not a duplicate. Wrong-location check: py-shiny is correct, this is Express-mode API surface. Regression check: not applicable, no prior behavior is broken. Reproduction plan: not applicable (feature request); verify by prototyping render.download_button/render.download_link per the 1:1 pairing pattern used for render.code(). Impact: quality-of-life improvement for Express-mode apps needing a download link instead of a button; narrow scope, no user currently blocked (workaround documented and functional). Priority: Low — well-defined, low-urgency API enhancement with no bug or deadline pressure. Recommended next action: implement per the proposal, resolving the two open questions raised in-thread (whether render.download stays as an alias, and whether to add icon/class_ pass-through referenced in #1230).

title: feat: Use the app author's navset id for data-tabsetid instead of a random value
rationale: Facts: MEMBER schloerke (self-filed) proposes deriving navset `data-tabsetid`/pane ids from the app author's own module-resolved `id=` instead of a random integer, citing #2296 (which already widened the random-ID pool after a birthday-paradox collision documented in #2285) as prior mitigation for the collision risk this would eliminate for id'd navsets. Includes a working prototype diff, validated locally against all 13 Playwright nav tests. Classification hypothesis: feature/internal-quality enhancement, not a live bug (the collision risk is already mitigated by #2296's larger ID pool). Duplicate check: searched for 'tabsetid' — only this issue; not a duplicate of #2285/#2296, which addressed the collision differently (bigger random pool) and remain the immediate fix. Wrong-location check: explicitly addressed in the issue body — py-shiny is correct since tabset ID generation is native Python code, not vendored from bslib; the author notes a parallel R-side issue could optionally be filed separately. Regression check: not applicable. Reproduction plan: not applicable; verify via the existing Playwright suite under tests/playwright/shiny/components/nav/ plus the provided prototype diff in shiny/ui/_navs.py. Impact: purely cosmetic/debuggability (readable, deterministic DOM ids) and minor test-snapshot stability; the correctness issue that originally motivated interest here (#2285 collision) is already resolved by #2296. Priority: Low — nice-to-have internal-quality improvement, not blocking or user-facing-broken. Recommended next action: land as a follow-up polish PR once the maintainer has bandwidth; flag the snapshot churn (existing test_navs.py expectations) called out in the issue as the main implementation cost.

title: docs: Add an example app and/or docs article for test mode
rationale: Facts: MEMBER schloerke (self-filed) flags that the test-mode feature shipped in #2269/PR #2270 (SHINY_TESTMODE, export_test_values(), the /dataobj/shinytest snapshot endpoint, and the AppTestValues Playwright controller) currently only has API docstrings, and requests an example app and/or docs article. Classification hypothesis: documentation task; 'needs clarification' label applied narrowly because the issue itself leaves the exact deliverable open ('and/or'), which is a scope decision for maintainers rather than missing repro/bug info. Duplicate check: distinct from #2269 (the feature) and from closed #2339/#2282 (a skill and snapshot-preprocessing feature); this is the first open docs-specific follow-up. Wrong-location check: py-shiny is correct, test mode is a py-shiny feature. Regression check: not applicable. Reproduction plan: not applicable (docs request). Impact: low — feature is already shipped and usable via docstrings; this only affects discoverability/onboarding for test mode. Priority: Low — documentation polish with no functional impact. Recommended next action: maintainers should decide whether to add an api-examples/ sample app, a docs article, or both, then scope a PR accordingly.

title: Support configuring context window size (`num_ctx`) for local models in `ChatOllama`
rationale: Facts: Reporter bgreenwell shows that ChatOllama talks to Ollama via the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint and that translate_model_params() strictly filters parameters, so there is no way to raise Ollama's default 2048-token context window (num_ctx), causing responses (especially from reasoning models) to truncate; includes a working R/ellmer comparison (chat_ollama() exposes num_ctx directly) and a failing Python reprex showing finish_reason == 'length'. Two concrete proposed fixes are given (expose num_ctx directly on ChatOllama, or pass through provider-specific keys in set_model_params() instead of raising TypeError). Classification hypothesis: feature gap / well-defined enhancement, not a bug in existing documented behavior. Duplicate check: searched chatlas issues for 'num_ctx'/'ChatOllama options' — no existing issue. Wrong-location check: chatlas is correct, ChatOllama and translate_model_params() live here. Regression check: not applicable, this was never supported. Reproduction plan: run the provided Python reprex (ChatOllama(model=...).set_model_params(max_tokens=2048), prompt long enough to exceed 2048 tokens) and observe finish_reason == 'length'; compare against the working ellmer::chat_ollama(params=params(num_ctx=8192)) call. Impact: blocks local-model users (a meaningful chatlas audience, given Ollama is the primary local-inference backend) from using larger-context or reasoning models without truncation; no documented workaround exists today short of patching translate_model_params(). Priority: Medium — well-defined, reproducible gap with real usability impact for local-model users, matching a valid feature request with moderate impact per the rubric. Recommended next action: expose num_ctx (or a generic options passthrough) on ChatOllama and relax set_model_params() to forward unrecognized provider-specific keys instead of raising, mirroring ellmer's existing behavior for parity.

title: Feature: standalone register_slash_command() for chat_ui() without chat_server()
rationale: Facts: Reporter kaipingyang references a standing TODO in chat_app.R and the Get started vignette's documented limitation that slash commands only work through chat_server()'s $slash_command() method, not with a fully custom chat loop built on chat_ui()/chat_append(). Proposes an exported register_slash_command(id, name, description, handler, ..., session) free function backed by session$userData (the same idiom already used for bookmark info in chat_restore.R), with the slash-dispatch logic factored into a shared internal helper reused by chat_server() (a pure refactor, no behavior change). States a draft PR is ready with standalone tests and a clean R CMD check. Classification hypothesis: feature, closing a documented gap. Duplicate check: searched for 'slash_command' — no other open or closed issue; not a duplicate. Wrong-location check: shinychat is correct, chat_app.R/chat_restore.R and the slash-command machinery live here. Regression check: not applicable. Reproduction plan: not applicable (feature); verify via the reporter's draft PR and its standalone tests once submitted. Impact: unblocks fully custom chat loops (chat_ui() + custom server logic, e.g. external agent loops) from using the official slash-command palette at all, and removes the need to manually thread chat_server()'s return value for simpler apps — a real, currently-undocumented-workaround friction point called out in the project's own vignette. Priority: Medium — well-defined request addressing a documented limitation, with moderate impact and a ready implementation, matching the rubric's medium-priority guidance. Recommended next action: review and merge the reporter's draft PR; resolve the open API question (return an unregister closure vs. a separate remove_slash_command() for parity with the Python Chat.remove_slash_command()) before merging.
